urgent_methodology: Publisher-marked Breaking, Urgent, Alert, Just in, Developing or similar items are collected first and receive priority over standard items, unless they are liveblogs, programme previews or low-value content.

latest.title: Kim Jong Un was meant to be their only idol - then North Koreans discovered K-pop
latest.summary: Lee Yeon-su took a train from Seoul to Busan for a BTS concert, expressing her joy at being able to support artists freely. She was born in North Korea, where attending events was restricted and music from the South was a crime.
latest.details: Lee Yeon-su took a train from Seoul to Busan for a BTS concert, expressing her joy at being able to support artists freely. She was born in North Korea, where attending events was restricted and music from the South was a crime. After escaping in 2011, she discovered K-pop, which helped her adjust to life in South Korea. Other defectors also reported secretly listening to K-pop in North Korea, finding hope in its lyrics.
latest.full_brief: Lee Yeon-su took a train from Seoul to Busan for a BTS concert, expressing her joy at being able to support artists freely. She was born in North Korea, where attending events was restricted and music from the South was a crime. After escaping in 2011, she discovered K-pop, which helped her adjust to life in South Korea. Other defectors also reported secretly listening to K-pop in North Korea, finding hope in its lyrics.

latest.title: Taco Bell removes lettuce from menu in US after links to explosive diarrhoea parasite
latest.summary: Taco Bell has removed shredded iceberg lettuce from its menu in the US due to a cyclosporiasis outbreak linked to the product. The CDC reported that 1,645 people in five states have been infected, with 94 hospitalizations.
latest.details: Taco Bell has removed shredded iceberg lettuce from its menu in the US due to a cyclosporiasis outbreak linked to the product. The CDC reported that 1,645 people in five states have been infected, with 94 hospitalizations. The FDA advised against consuming the affected lettuce, which is sourced from Mexico. Taco Bell's supplier, Taylor Farms, is voluntarily removing the lettuce from the market.
latest.full_brief: Taco Bell has removed shredded iceberg lettuce from its menu in the US due to a cyclosporiasis outbreak linked to the product. The CDC reported that 1,645 people in five states have been infected, with 94 hospitalizations. The FDA advised against consuming the affected lettuce, which is sourced from Mexico. Taco Bell's supplier, Taylor Farms, is voluntarily removing the lettuce from the market.

latest.title: Trump threatens to tariff Canada over wildfire smoke choking northern US cities
latest.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to impose tariffs on Canada due to wildfires causing smoke to choke northern US cities. As of Friday, there were approximately 888 active wildfires in Canada, with many burning out of control.
latest.details: US President Donald Trump has threatened to impose tariffs on Canada due to wildfires causing smoke to choke northern US cities. As of Friday, there were approximately 888 active wildfires in Canada, with many burning out of control. Trump accused Canada of 'willful negligence' in forest management and stated that the US is suffering from unhealthy air quality. Canadian officials have not responded to the tariff threat but emphasized the shared responsibility in addressing climate change.
latest.full_brief: US President Donald Trump has threatened to impose tariffs on Canada due to wildfires causing smoke to choke northern US cities. As of Friday, there were approximately 888 active wildfires in Canada, with many burning out of control. Trump accused Canada of 'willful negligence' in forest management and stated that the US is suffering from unhealthy air quality. Canadian officials have not responded to the tariff threat but emphasized the shared responsibility in addressing climate change.

latest.title: More Canadian wildfire smoke shrouds US midwest, mid-Atlantic and north-east
latest.summary: Canadian wildfire smoke has affected about 109 million people across the US, leading to poor air quality alerts. Cities like Chicago and Detroit experienced hazardous air quality levels, prompting residents to stay indoors.
latest.details: Canadian wildfire smoke has affected about 109 million people across the US, leading to poor air quality alerts. Cities like Chicago and Detroit experienced hazardous air quality levels, prompting residents to stay indoors. The smoke has also reached Baltimore and Washington DC, with air quality readings classified as very unhealthy. The situation has led to significant disruptions, including the cancellation of outdoor events.
latest.full_brief: Canadian wildfire smoke has affected about 109 million people across the US, leading to poor air quality alerts. Cities like Chicago and Detroit experienced hazardous air quality levels, prompting residents to stay indoors. The smoke has also reached Baltimore and Washington DC, with air quality readings classified as very unhealthy. The situation has led to significant disruptions, including the cancellation of outdoor events.

latest.title: Many Ukrainian soldiers outraged over removal of defence minister, troops tell BBC
latest.summary: Ukrainian soldiers have expressed outrage over President Zelensky's decision not to re-appoint Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Many soldiers feel this decision is a betrayal and a slap in the face to service members.
latest.details: Ukrainian soldiers have expressed outrage over President Zelensky's decision not to re-appoint Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Many soldiers feel this decision is a betrayal and a slap in the face to service members. Despite protests, some soldiers doubt that public anger will lead to any change. Fedorov is credited with driving military innovation, particularly in the use of drones, which has been crucial in the ongoing conflict.
latest.full_brief: Ukrainian soldiers have expressed outrage over President Zelensky's decision not to re-appoint Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Many soldiers feel this decision is a betrayal and a slap in the face to service members. Despite protests, some soldiers doubt that public anger will lead to any change. Fedorov is credited with driving military innovation, particularly in the use of drones, which has been crucial in the ongoing conflict.

latest.title: EU proposes slowing down cuts to carbon emissions for businesses
latest.summary: The European Union has proposed reforms to slow down cuts to greenhouse gas emissions limits for businesses. These changes would allow some industries to obtain emission allowances until 2038, extending the timeline for carbon output reduction.
latest.details: The European Union has proposed reforms to slow down cuts to greenhouse gas emissions limits for businesses. These changes would allow some industries to obtain emission allowances until 2038, extending the timeline for carbon output reduction. The proposals are still subject to approval by EU countries and lawmakers, which may take up to a year. The European Commission aims to align these changes with the EU's goal of reducing carbon emissions by 90% by 2040.
latest.full_brief: The European Union has proposed reforms to slow down cuts to greenhouse gas emissions limits for businesses. These changes would allow some industries to obtain emission allowances until 2038, extending the timeline for carbon output reduction. The proposals are still subject to approval by EU countries and lawmakers, which may take up to a year. The European Commission aims to align these changes with the EU's goal of reducing carbon emissions by 90% by 2040.

latest.title: Robodebt whistleblower was told her royal commission evidence ‘could cost you your job’, court…
latest.summary: Jeannie-Marie Blake was a key whistleblower in the robodebt scandal, exposing issues with the scheme while still working for the government. She alleges that her department made repeated threats against her before and after her testimony at the royal commission.
latest.details: Jeannie-Marie Blake was a key whistleblower in the robodebt scandal, exposing issues with the scheme while still working for the government. She alleges that her department made repeated threats against her before and after her testimony at the royal commission. Blake was the only current Services Australia employee to give voluntary testimony, which was crucial to the investigation. She has expressed that the trauma from the scheme has left her battling severe mental health issues.
latest.full_brief: Jeannie-Marie Blake was a key whistleblower in the robodebt scandal, exposing issues with the scheme while still working for the government. She alleges that her department made repeated threats against her before and after her testimony at the royal commission. Blake was the only current Services Australia employee to give voluntary testimony, which was crucial to the investigation. She has expressed that the trauma from the scheme has left her battling severe mental health issues.

latest.title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
latest.summary: The Republic of Mauritius has officially joined the Artemis Accords, becoming the seventh African country to do so. NASA's Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson welcomed Mauritius during a signing ceremony in Ébène.
latest.details: The Republic of Mauritius has officially joined the Artemis Accords, becoming the seventh African country to do so. NASA's Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson welcomed Mauritius during a signing ceremony in Ébène. The Permanent Secretary of Mauritius signed the accords, emphasizing the country's commitment to responsible space governance. NASA's engagement with Mauritius dates back to its global mapping efforts between 1965 and 1980.
latest.full_brief: The Republic of Mauritius has officially joined the Artemis Accords, becoming the seventh African country to do so. NASA's Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson welcomed Mauritius during a signing ceremony in Ébène. The Permanent Secretary of Mauritius signed the accords, emphasizing the country's commitment to responsible space governance. NASA's engagement with Mauritius dates back to its global mapping efforts between 1965 and 1980.

latest.title: Distillery owner could face only three months in jail over alleged poisoning Australian…
latest.summary: A distillery owner in Laos could face only three months in jail for charges related to the poisoning deaths of Australian backpackers. The court announced charges for selling harmful food and operating an illegal business, with a potential prison sentence of three months to four years.
latest.details: A distillery owner in Laos could face only three months in jail for charges related to the poisoning deaths of Australian backpackers. The court announced charges for selling harmful food and operating an illegal business, with a potential prison sentence of three months to four years. The case remains open for 15 years, allowing for more serious charges if sufficient evidence arises. Families of the victims expressed disappointment over the light charges, seeking justice for their loved ones.
latest.full_brief: A distillery owner in Laos could face only three months in jail for charges related to the poisoning deaths of Australian backpackers. The court announced charges for selling harmful food and operating an illegal business, with a potential prison sentence of three months to four years. The case remains open for 15 years, allowing for more serious charges if sufficient evidence arises. Families of the victims expressed disappointment over the light charges, seeking justice for their loved ones.

latest.title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
latest.summary: In April 2026, NASA’s Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer initiated a working group to address the risk of venous thromboembolism (VTE) during spaceflight. This group was formed after diagnosing VTEs in astronauts during ISS missions.
latest.details: In April 2026, NASA’s Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer initiated a working group to address the risk of venous thromboembolism (VTE) during spaceflight. This group was formed after diagnosing VTEs in astronauts during ISS missions. The working group reviewed updated case information and discussed new evidence-based clinical practice recommendations.
latest.full_brief: In April 2026, NASA’s Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer initiated a working group to address the risk of venous thromboembolism (VTE) during spaceflight. This group was formed after diagnosing VTEs in astronauts during ISS missions. The working group reviewed updated case information and discussed new evidence-based clinical practice recommendations.

brief_methodology.pl: Komentarz powstaje wyłącznie z przeczytanego tekstu artykułu. Musi przejść niezależny przegląd AI oraz pełną kontrolę językową; surowy tekst artykułu lub RSS nie może być komentarzem awaryjnym.
brief_methodology.en: A comment is created only from the retrieved article text. It must pass an independent AI review and the complete language gate; raw article or RSS text is never a fallback comment.

comment_quality_gate.pl: Publikowany jest wyłącznie komentarz utworzony z tekstu artykułu, zatwierdzony w niezależnym przeglądzie AI i przyjęty w całości przez rygorystyczną kontrolę językową. Jedno wadliwe zdanie odrzuca cały komentarz.
comment_quality_gate.en: Only an article-derived comment approved by an independent AI review and accepted in full by the strict language gate is published. One defective sentence rejects the whole comment.

same_story_dedupe.rule: One underlying event gets one homepage card, even if multiple publishers cover it with different links.
